page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-8 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 4 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores with 2 threads per core. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this configuration, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying AMD EPYC processor provides a 32 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 (specifically avx512f, avx512_vnni, and avx512_bf16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core execution and Geekbench text processing. Multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and ray tracing benchmarks fall within the average tier.

While cached small-block memory read bandwidth is average at 566.42 GB/sec, the instance exhibits a performance tradeoff in uncached memory operations. Large-block read bandwidth drops to a weak 36.85 GB/sec, and large-block write bandwidth is poor at 13.83 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested configurations. This indicates potential bottlenecks for memory-intensive workloads that exceed cache limits.

Given the compute-heavy 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong single-core performance, this instance is suited for compute-bound tasks such as web serving, text processing, and lightweight software compilation. It can handle basic database operations and small-scale language model inference, but is constrained for memory-bound applications or large-scale LLM processing due to its limited memory capacity and uncached memory write speeds.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It provides 4 physical cores with multithreading, a 32 MB L3 cache, and hardware support for AVX-512 and SHA_NI. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and text processing capabilities, though multi-core execution and software compilation remain average. A key tradeoff is its uncached memory performance, where large-block write bandwidth falls into the bottom 10%. This resource profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is suited for web serving, text processing, and lightweight compilation tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance with 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance but limited uncached memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-8 features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized configuration featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, strong single-core performance, and a balanced compute-to-memory ratio.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 9B14.
Strong single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance.
AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration enabled.
Tradeoff of lower uncached large-block memory write bandwidth.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Suited for web serving, text processing, and light compilation.
